attacking_style: Pure sprinter who excels in chaotic, disorganized finales where lead-out trains are disrupted. Thrives when positioning skills matter more than raw power or team support.
team_tactics: Historically reliant on lead-out support (Cofidis setup in 2020), but demonstrated ability to operate independently when organizational structure collapses due to crashes or late-race chaos.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages are primary territory. Vulnerable on selective terrain, though 2021 Giro second place suggests emerging capabilities when form peaks on moderately difficult stages.
vulnerability: Positioning failures in standard bunch sprints when lead-out trains remain intact (2019 Giro third). Crash susceptibility in high-speed finales (2020 Giro elimination). Struggles when organization matters more than improvisation.
key_patterns: Excels in disrupted sprints after crashes eliminate lead-out structure (2020 crash victim, 2023 winner).
Positioning issues in standard organized bunch kicks: third from peloton when expected higher (2019).
Form-dependent results: peaks enable unexpected placement on selective terrain (2021 advancing group second).
Can win without lead-out support when chaos levels playing field for all sprinters (2023).
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Vuelta stage win shows matured ability to capitalize on chaos; successful transition from lead-out dependency to self-sufficiency
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 19
2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 19
